semanticCore: Heavy rain, evil consequence, gravity, severity, or pasture
quranicRelevance: Heavy torrential rain (Wabil - 'fa-asabaha wabilun fatarakahu saldan'), and tasting the evil consequence of deeds (Wabala - 'fathaqat wabala amriha').

keyDerivations.meaning: Heavy rain, downpour, or torrential shower
Evil consequence, gravity, weight, severity, or toxicity of results

figurativeOrRareUsages: فَذَاقَتْ وَبَالَ أَمْرِهَا (65:9): So it (the community) tasted the evil consequence/severity of its conduct (the heavy moral toll of disobedience).
discardedNoise: Plague and epidemic (Wabaa) - written with hamza (وَبَأ), distinct etymology.
summaryEn: The root connects meteorological weight (Wabil - heavy downpour) with moral consequence (Wabal), illustrating how actions return as a heavy, crushing harvest.
summaryAr: يربط هذا الجذر بين المطر الثقيل الغزير (وابل)، والجزاء الثقيل الوبيل للسيئات (وبال أمرها)، مصوراً عواقب الذنوب كحمل خانق.
rawText: بُلَةٌ : see إِبَالَةٌ. وَابِلٌ : see وَبْلٌ. وَابِلَةٌ The extremity [in which is the glenoid cavity] of the scapula: and the portion of flesh [or muscle] of the scapula. (IAar, T.) See مَرْدَغَةٌ, and also ضَدَفٌ; and more particularly اِنْفَرَكَ. وَبَالٌ An evil result. (Msb.) وَبَلَ 1 He (a horse) ran vehemently: see an ex. in a verse cited voce دَامَ, in art. دوم. وَبَلَةٌ : see أَبَلَةٌ. وَبِيلَةٌ : see إِبَالَةٌ. وَبْلٌ Violent rain, consisting of large drops; as also 🡻وَابِلٌ; (K;) a heavy rain.
